findings.title: PROP-073 detective layer reveals 20% invocation rate (1/5) over 5 post-deploy workspace-sync cycles
findings.description: Post-PROP-073 deployment at commit 15674330 (2026-05-31T21:31:25Z), 5 workspace-sync cycles ran: 22:09, 23:19, 00:11, 01:20, 02:09. Of these, ONLY the 00:11 cycle invoked Step 4c (sentinel file sync-workspace-runs-2026-06-01T00-10-15Z.json present in commit ecafbc1; agent_notes contains step4c-invoked + sentinel write path + GIT_APPEND_ONLY canary block). The other 4 cycles produced minimal-schema run reports with no step4c field at all. Sub-fix #1 (stage-delta gating) is working as designed — ZERO mis-titled "PROP-066 step4c sentinel" commits in the post-deploy window (vs 5 in the pre-deploy window). The pathology has shifted from "silent lying commits" to "silent no-op runs." Strict PROP-074 trigger threshold (0/3 in next 3h) was NOT met — the first 3-cycle window post-deploy (22:09, 23:19, 00:11) had 1 invocation, not 0. Every 3-cycle window across the 5 cycles has exactly 1 invocation. By the letter of the prior trigger, PROP-074 should not be authored.
evidence: Sentinel files in monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-*.json: only 1 in the 5-cycle window (sync-workspace-runs-2026-06-01T00-10-15Z.json)

Workspace-sync run reports — agent_notes field per cycle: 22:09 "routine; soft-complaint: narrative-cite..." (NO step4c); 23:19 "routine soft-complaint..." (NO step4c); 00:11 "step4c-invoked; sentinel written to..." (HAS step4c); 01:20 "soft-complaint: narrative-cite..." (NO step4c); 02:09 "routine sync: 8 files committed..." (NO step4c)

Two distinct report schemas observed: 00:11 used a comprehensive schema with commit_sha + files_new/modified/deleted/skipped + step4c_invoked + step4c_sentinel + git_append_only_canaries; the 4 skip cycles used a minimal schema with only run_id + timestamp + files_committed + skips_total + skip_breakdown + agent_notes + cleanup_ran. Same prompt should be producing the same schema; this divergence is itself a signal the LLM agent is not consistently following the post-PROP-072 instructions.
Zero "PROP-066 step4c sentinel" commit titles post-deploy (forensic: c9e1d92 / 007d0fe / b7c36cb / fb52feb8 / f1def7e were the pre-deploy mis-titled commits; sub-fix #1 successfully eliminated them).
findings.counterargument: One could read this as PROP-073 working AND working: the detective layer made the failure visible (we now have ground-truth data on invocation rate), and the strict 0/3 threshold deliberately calibrated by the prior tinker run is not met. Acting now would be jumping the gun against an explicit operator-set escalation criterion. PROP-074 is a structural change to the agent fleet (new dome-step4c-sync scheduled task) and warrants operator deliberation, not unilateral authoring.
findings.recommended_action: Surface to operator via latest-tinker-summary.txt. HOLD on PROP-074 authoring this run. If the next 3 workspace-sync cycles (03:03, 04:03, 05:03 UTC) all skip Step 4c, that is a clean 0/3 window and the threshold fires — next tinker run (03:30 UTC tomorrow) or operator should author PROP-074. If 1+ of the next 3 invokes, hold pattern continues.
findings.verification_pattern: cd /tmp/tinker-clone && N=0 && echo "sentinels_last_8:  (target ≥5/8 = 62.5% to declare invocation reliable)"

findings.title: workspace-sync run reports use two different schemas — symptom of the same invocation reliability problem
findings.description: Comparing the 00:11 report (run-2026-06-01T00-11-25Z.json) to 01:20 and 02:09 reports shows two completely different report schemas. The 00:11 report has run_ts + agent + commit_sha + files_new/modified/deleted/skipped + push_status + step4c_invoked + step4c_sentinel + git_append_only_canaries + agent_notes + notes. The 01:20 and 02:09 reports have only run_id + timestamp + files_committed + skips_total + skip_breakdown + agent_notes + cleanup_ran. The newer comprehensive schema appears to correspond to the LLM correctly following the post-PROP-073 prompt; the older minimal schema suggests the LLM is partially or wholly skipping the prompt and falling back to a simpler procedure. This is the structural-not-behavioral version of the same problem FND-01 describes. Worth noting in operator decision on PROP-074: structural separation would standardize the schema by definition (the dedicated step4c agent would always emit the comprehensive schema).
findings.recommended_action: No action this run. The schema divergence is a symptom of FND-01, not a separate problem. PROP-074 (if authored) would fix this as a side effect.

findings.title: Root FS at 96% used at run start (414MB free); project footprint 728MB above 500MB warning threshold
findings.description: df -h / showed 9.6G total, 9.2G used, 414MB available at clone time. Project-induced footprint (FUSE workspace 399MB + /tmp/tinker-clone 312MB + small /tmp scratch) = 728MB, above the 500MB warning threshold. No stale /tmp/*-clone leftovers — only our active /tmp/tinker-clone at 312MB (mtime age 0h). The standing empowerment for autonomous /tmp/*-clone cleanup requires age >24h; none of the present clones qualify. Mandatory end-of-run cleanup will reclaim 312MB and drop project footprint back to ~416MB (below warning). Consistent with prior tinker FND-03 (2026-05-31T21-34: 96% used, 443MB free). Pattern suggests the sandbox baseline has crept upward over recent days.
findings.no_action_reason: No actionable stale clones; end-of-run cleanup is the relief valve.
findings.verification_pattern: df -h / | awk "NR==2 {print }" | tr -d % | awk "{if(>=95) print "STILL_BROKEN"; else print "FIXED"}"

findings.title: curmudgeon-verify (Sonnet, 4h cadence, enabled) has produced 1 review total since launch and 0 in last 7d
findings.description: Scanning monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/*.json: 12 reviews have an agent_subtype field set; exactly 1 has agent_subtype=curmudgeon-verify; that 1 is older than 7d. The agent is scheduled (every 4h offset from main curmudgeon) and enabled. Two possible explanations: (a) the dispatch criterion (class=verification AND ≤2 minor prior holes AND patches landed since prior review) genuinely matches no items in the queue; (b) the agent has a bug preventing it from claiming work. Cheap to investigate (read its latest summary file). Not urgent — the agent is Sonnet so cost is low even if pure no-op; but baby_drain (1/7d) and verify_drain (0/7d) both running near-empty suggests we are over-provisioned on consolidation-class agents post HOL Phase 2 drain.
findings.recommended_action: Defer to next Mode 3 run. If still 0/7d in next observation window, downgrade curmudgeon-verify schedule from 4h to daily, or convert it into an event-driven on-demand task.
